mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% signals an extended zero-lower-bound monetary regime. Sustained low rates compress equity risk premiums and reduce discount rates on future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 143,048 thousand persons combined with unemployment rate at 6.2% indicates labor market slack. Rising slack signals weakening aggregate demand and future earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances (UNRATE > 6.0%), or the correlation between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is positive (r > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 568,215 million dollars combined with housing starts at 1,580 thousand units indicate consumer and residential demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to earnings growth expectations, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 52% of co-growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 107.18 (index 2017=100) reflects supply-side capacity utilization. Declining industrial production signals supply constraints and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 263.161 (index 1982-1984=100) combined with federal funds rate at 0.08% creates a deeply negative real rate environment. This regime persists when nominal rates remain below inflation expectations, supporting equity valuations through reduced real discount rates and producing positive 60-day returns.
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the real rate proxy (FEDFUNDS - CPI inflation) shows positive correlation with forward returns (r > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 18,783.902 billion chained 2017 dollars represents the broadest demand signal. Quarterly GDP growth above 2% annualized signals robust economic expansion and supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods with above-2% GDP growth, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 143,048 thousand persons shows the absolute level of labor market size. Declining payroll employment signals recession risk and earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when PAYEMS falls month-over-month.
regime_hint: labor market contraction or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months with declining payroll employment, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 107.18 combined with payroll employment at 143,048 thousand indicates joint supply and labor capacity. When both series decline simultaneously, the economy faces stagflationary pressure with constrained supply and shrinking labor input, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
regime_hint: stagflationary supply shock with labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of co-decline instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
